story: Over drinks, Murad smokes and listens while I tell him the childhood memory of following a man called Nico into a vacant apartment. Later, in the taxi, at my building, he asks—as always—to come up. I refuse: I don’t sleep with married men. I go inside and whisper the truth I never admit to anyone: I don’t sleep with anyone. We keep seeing each other; the next time we ride in silence and the cab rolls to my entrance.
question: When the cab stops at her entrance on their next outing, what will Murad most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Refrain from asking and simply say good night and leave.
answer_without_knowledge: Ask again to come up to her apartment.
